_readme: The .uetkx scanner contract corpus (D-22): run by BOTH the C++ automation test
(ReactiveUI.Uetkx.Scanner) and lsp-server's node --test — the family mechanism that keeps
two implementations in two languages in lockstep (scanner-cases.json precedent).
Every `at`/`expect` value is a Unicode CODE POINT index (D-18) — the nonBmp cases exist
to catch any implementation indexing by UTF-16 units.
skipNoncode = C++ lexis. skipNoncodeMarkup/findMatchingMarkup = the family markup lexis.

_doc: Corpus mirroring partition (A4 / TD-009). familyCore sections are BYTE-IDENTICAL
family-wide after the code-prefix substitution (UETKX|GUITKX|UITKX -> TKX) and are
hashed by scripts/corpus-hash.mjs into plans/family-corpus.hash; sibling repos adopt
the same script + hash file (release-time hash-match resolves TD-009). perLeg sections
are engine-specific lexis (C++/GDScript/C#) and are NOT part of the family hash.
nonBmp cases are routed by their per-case `section` field into whichever tier owns it.

skipNoncode.name: digit separator apostrophe is NOT a char
prefix after identifier is not a prefix
R after identifier is not raw

skipNoncodeMarkup.name: hash is literal text in markup

findMatchingMarkup.name: component body: prelude comment with paren does not desync
if header is code then body braces
markup text hash inside return window line classified prelude
code island bracket inherits code lexis

nonBmp.name: emoji in string counts as ONE code point (skipNoncode)

_fileScanDoc: fileScan cases run FUetkxFileScan::Scan (C++) / scanFile (TS) over a whole .uetkx source and
assert the parsed shape: imports (names+specifier), components/hooks/modules (name+exported),
the source `order`, and the exact set of diagnostic CODES. `basename` defaults to "" (so the
component/file-name nudge 0103 never fires). `noError` asserts hasError()==false (LINT-only).
fileScan = family-core import/export/mixed grammar (byte-identical family-wide); fileScanLeg =
per-leg hook casing (Unreal/Unity PascalCase Use*, Godot snake_case use_*).
ES-MODULES DRAFT (M0.4, plans/ES_MODULES_EXECUTION_PLAN.md — NOT YET IMPLEMENTED, prospective
hash only): import.localNames (rename target; omitted when == names), import.namespace
(`* as X` local alias), import.default (default-import local alias), defaultExport (the
file's `export default <Name>` target, if any) are new fileScan fields. `values`/`utils`
arrays + `value:`/`util:` order entries are new decl-kind shapes. New-form typed
component/hook/value/util declarations are per-leg (fileScanLeg) — G-04 native-idiom typing
means their HEAD grammar is not byte-identical family-wide (GDScript's trailing `-> Type` vs
C++/C# leading-type head); only the wrapper-keyword deprecation (2320) and the import/export
family-core, since those are engine-neutral syntax. 2321/2322 need native type spelling to
trigger (per-leg). 2326 (no default export) is a cross-file RESOLVER check, not a scanner
diagnostic — it is corpus-tested via ContractFixtures/resolver tests instead, not here.

fileScan.name: import one name
import multiple names, ~/ root specifier
import parent-relative specifier
import non-BMP specifier keeps code points intact
multiple imports, mixed specifier forms
export component
private component (no export)
export module
fileScan.input: export module Styles { inline const float Pad = 4.0f; }

fileScan.name: mixed component + module preserves source order
duplicate import is UETKX2303
import after a declaration is UETKX2309
two components is the one-per-file convention warn UETKX2105 (not an error)
import-only file (no declaration) is UETKX2101
fileScan.input: import { A } from "./X"

fileScan.name: empty import list is UETKX0303
wave G: early returns — multiple markup returns accepted (last top-level anchors)
wave G: every return window parses per-span — a bad EARLY window still diagnoses
S4/markup-everywhere (family): markup as a value scans clean — assignment + paren boundaries
S5/es-modules: rename import (`as`)
S5/es-modules: rename import mixed with a plain name in one brace list
S5/es-modules: namespace import (`* as`)
S5/es-modules: default import
S5/es-modules: deferred export list (`export { a, b };`)
S5/es-modules: export list is mixable with inline `export`
S5/es-modules: `export default Name;` does not implicitly export it for named import
S5/es-modules 2320: `component` wrapper is deprecated
S5/es-modules 2320: `module` wrapper is deprecated

S5/es-modules 2325: import alias collides with a local declaration
S5/es-modules 2325: namespace import alias collides with another import's local name
S5/es-modules 2327: duplicate `export default`

fileScanLeg.name: private hook (no export)

fileScanLeg.name: full mixed sequence: component + hook + module in source order
hook before component keeps declaration order
wave G: final markup return must be top-level (UETKX3007)
wave G: lambda brace-init return stays verbatim C++ (not a markup window)
include retirement: host include + file import mix parse together
include retirement: import string without leading @ is UETKX0303
include retirement: duplicate host include payload is UETKX2303
include retirement: a prelude-covered host include is UETKX2317 (hint, not an error)
TB-5/0107: plain early return dims the rest of the body (hint, not an error)
TB-5/0107: statements after the final markup return are unreachable
TB-5/0107: a CONDITIONAL early return is reachable code -- no diagnostic
S4/markup-everywhere: markup as a VALUE (auto X = (<Tag>...)) scans clean
S4/0114 narrowed: a paren-less markup return is the one still-illegal spelling

S4/rules-of-hooks 0014: hook inside a loop

S4/rules-of-hooks 0016: hook inside an event attr
S4/HMR protection: hook names inside value-markup text never enter the hook signature

fileScanLeg.name: ES combined: default + named list parse as ONE declaration (rename kept)
ES combined: default + namespace star parse as ONE declaration
